title: Maple and pecan cake
subtitle: A warm spiced sponge with maple syrup and a maple cream cheese frosting
excerpt: A two-layer spiced butter cake flavoured with maple syrup and folded with chopped pecans, filled and topped with a maple cream cheese frosting and pecan halves.
sourceNotes: Maple and pecan as a cake combination draws on Canadian and American baking traditions. Maple syrup in baking was documented in New England and Canadian household cooking from the early nineteenth century. The recipe here uses the standard British creaming method, adapted for the maple flavour documented in North American home-baking guides of the early twentieth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted layers. The maple cream cheese frosting does not freeze.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The sponge layers can be baked a day ahead. Assemble and frost on the day of serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: The reduced sap of maple trees. Grade A dark maple syrup has the most pronounced flavour for baking; lighter grades are milder and work well in more delicate cakes. Pure maple syrup is essential; maple-flavoured syrup is a corn syrup substitute with a very different flavour.

content.text: is a key ingredient here; do not substitute the maple-flavoured corn syrup sold as a pancake topping. The flavour is entirely different. Grade A dark amber or the old Grade B gives the strongest baking flavour.

Preheat the oven to 180°C conventional. Grease and line two 23 cm round tins. Beat the butter and brown sugar together until fluffy. Beat in the maple syrup, then the eggs one at a time. Fold in the flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and nutmeg in alternation with the milk. Fold in the toasted pecans. Divide between the tins and bake for 25 to 30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool on racks.

Beat the cream cheese and butter until smooth. Add the sifted icing sugar in two additions and beat until thick. Beat in the maple syrup. If the frosting is too soft, refrigerate for 15 minutes. Place the first layer on a serving plate and spread with one third of the frosting. Top with the second layer and cover the top and sides with the remaining frosting. Arrange the pecan halves over the top.
